title: auth — subscribed events
description: Auth is BOTH a heavy publisher AND a heavy listener. It reacts to identity-substrate events (session revocation on credential mutation) + tenancy events (session revocation on tenant erasure) + user events (session revocation on user suspension) + auth's own events (breach lockdown, JWT key rotation cache invalidation).

listeners.purpose: The canonical session-revocation-on-credential-mutation listener. Dispatches RevokeAllActiveSessionsJob — revokes every active PAT for every User referencing the Identity, cross-Application. Fires AllSessionsRevoked once with the aggregated count.
Same as PasswordChanged — disabling MFA is a security-material credential mutation. Same job, different trigger event.
Lockout revokes sessions to prevent an attacker who obtained a valid PAT from continuing to use it during the lockout window.
Per-Application suspension — revokes ONLY the sessions issued to this particular User (not cross-Application, unlike PasswordChanged). Fires AllSessionsRevoked with user_id set.
Same as UserSuspended — disabled Users cannot hold active sessions.

On IdentityErased(erasure_phase='soft_delete'), revoke every active session cross-Application. On erasure_phase='hard_delete', the cascade already fired (via onDelete=cascade on the FK) — this listener is idempotent.

The BREACH-response listener. On refresh reuse: invalidate every refresh token in the family_id chain + revoke every active PAT for the User + dispatch a security notification to the User + write a compliance-audit entry naming the reuse. Idempotent.
Synchronous cache eviction of auth:jwks:response. Downstream verifiers pulling the JWKS after this point receive the new key on their next refresh.

Publish the new deny-list entry via the platform's pubsub bus so every downstream service's auth.jwt_verify middleware refreshes its in-memory deny-list cache within seconds (faster than the natural 15-minute TTL).
On full-tenant erasure: revoke every active session for every User under that tenant. Fires AllSessionsRevoked with tenant_id set.

Synchronous cache eviction of auth:session_scope:token:{token_id} for every active PAT held by Users whose role/permission mutated. Ensures the next request from that PAT re-resolves permissions from the current RBAC snapshot (rather than the cached compact array from PAT issue-time).

Composes DeviceFingerprinter's per-User fingerprint cache. If the login's fingerprint doesn't match any seen in the last new_device_lookback_days, fires LoginFromNewDeviceDetected which the notification pipeline picks up.
Synchronous update of users.last_login_at + identities.last_login_at. Same transaction as the audit-log write so partial state is impossible.

notes: The RevokeAllActiveSessionsListener is bound to THREE events (PasswordChanged, MfaDisabled, IdentityLocked) because the underlying job (RevokeAllActiveSessionsJob) is the same for all three cases. The listener resolves the event's identity_id + dispatches the job.
The RevokeSessionsForUserListener is a SEPARATE listener because User-scoped revocation is user_id-scoped (not identity_id-scoped) — different logic path.
Every listener above is idempotent by design — retries after partial success don't double-fire downstream effects.

The auth::UserLoggedIn listeners are LOCAL to this module — UserLoggedIn fires alongside identity's IdentityRegistered but only auth listens (activity, observability, analytics also consume, per events.json).
